Title: AaMYC3 bridges the regulation of glandular trichome density and artemisinin biosynthesis in Artemisia annua

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: glandular-secreting trichomes (GSTs), especially young leaves and buds
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts downstream of AaMYC3 to promote GST initiation and thereby increases capacity for artemisinin accumulation/storage.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in young leaves; low in mature leaves; GST-associated
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in young leaves; low in mature leaves; induced in AaMYC3 overexpression lines and reduced in AaMYC3 RNAi lines
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pAaHD1::LUC reporter in tobacco transient assays
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; RNA-seq; dual-luciferase assay; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; CUT&Tag-qPCR
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: jasmonic acid (JA)/MeJA responsive GST developmental context
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaHD1 transcript levels increase in AaMYC3-OE and decrease in AaMYC3-RNAi plants. 2. AaMYC3 directly binds the G-box in the AaHD1 promoter and activates its transcription. 3. AaHD1 mediates the link between GST initiation and enhanced artemisinin production capacity.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be combined with AaMYC3-centered engineering to increase GST density and potentially boost artemisinin yield.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In this paper AaHD1 is mainly analyzed as a direct downstream target of AaMYC3 rather than as the primary focal gene.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms intermediate en route to target product
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Oxidized artemisinin-pathway intermediates leading toward artemisinic aldehyde/acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes sequential oxidations of amorpha-4,11-diene-derived intermediates in the artemisinin pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key pathway enzyme, not explicitly established here as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression is directly activated by AaMYC3, promoting DHAA and artemisinin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with AaMYC3 in bud trichomes/young tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in OE-AaMYC3 and downregulated in RNAi-AaMYC3
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pCYP71AV1::LUC reporter; AaMYC3 overexpression driven by double CaMV35S promoter
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; RNA-seq; dual-luciferase assay; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; CUT&Tag-qPCR; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for stacking with AaMYC3 or other positive regulators to improve artemisinin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71AV1 expression increases strongly in AaMYC3-OE lines and decreases in RNAi lines. 2. AaMYC3 directly binds an E-box in the CYP71AV1 promoter. 3. AaMYC3 activates CYP71AV1 transcription and contributes to higher DHAA/artemisinin levels.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms direct precursor pool toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes artemisinin-pathway aldehydes to the corresponding acids, contributing to DHAA formation toward artemisinin.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key pathway enzyme, not explicitly established here as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression is directly activated by AaMYC3, promoting DHAA and artemisinin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with AaMYC3 in bud trichomes/young tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in OE-AaMYC3 and downregulated in RNAi-AaMYC3
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pALDH1::LUC reporter; AaMYC3 overexpression driven by double CaMV35S promoter
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; RNA-seq; dual-luciferase assay; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; CUT&Tag-qPCR; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful engineering target in artemisinin pathway and a validated direct target of AaMYC3.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ALDH1 expression rises in AaMYC3-OE lines and falls in RNAi lines. 2. AaMYC3 directly binds an E-box in the ALDH1 promoter. 3. Activation of ALDH1 by AaMYC3 supports increased DHAA/artemisinin accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Committed entry step of artemisinin-specific sesquiterpenoid branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms the first committed intermediate toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes farnesyl diphosphate to amorpha-4,11-diene, the first committed intermediate of artemisinin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transcript abundance is positively associated with AaMYC3, but AaMYC3 regulates ADS indirectly by co-activating AabHLH1 rather than binding the promoter itself.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with AaMYC3 in trichome-rich tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in OE-AaMYC3 and downregulated in RNAi-AaMYC3
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pADS::LUC reporter; AaMYC3 overexpression driven by double CaMV35S promoter
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; RNA-seq; dual-luciferase assay; Y1H negative binding test for AaMYC3; EMSA showing enhanced AabHLH1 binding in presence of AaMYC3; interaction assays with AabHLH1
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A prime enzyme target for pathway stacking; this paper suggests stronger activation may require AaMYC3 together with AabHLH1.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ADS expression changes positively with AaMYC3 level in transgenic plants. 2. AaMYC3 alone does not bind or activate the ADS promoter. 3. AaMYC3 interacts with AabHLH1 and enhances AabHLH1-mediated ADS promoter activation.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: diverts flux toward target product branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces artemisinic aldehyde to dihydroartemisinic aldehyde, directing flux toward dihydroartemisinic acid and artemisinin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transcript abundance is positively associated with AaMYC3, but AaMYC3 regulates DBR2 indirectly by co-activating AabHLH113 rather than binding the promoter itself.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with AaMYC3 in trichome-rich tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in OE-AaMYC3 and downregulated in RNAi-AaMYC3
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pDBR2::LUC reporter; AaMYC3 overexpression driven by double CaMV35S promoter
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; RNA-seq; dual-luciferase assay; Y1H negative binding test for AaMYC3; EMSA showing enhanced AabHLH113 binding in presence of AaMYC3; interaction assays with AabHLH113
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising branch-control target; this paper suggests co-expression with AaMYC3 plus AabHLH113 may better boost artemisinin flux.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DBR2 expression changes positively with AaMYC3 level in transgenic plants. 2. AaMYC3 alone does not bind or activate the DBR2 promoter. 3. AaMYC3 interacts with AabHLH113 and enhances AabHLH113-mediated DBR2 promoter activation.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms transcriptional co-activation complexes with AabHLH1 and AabHLH113
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation of AaHD1, CYP71AV1 and ALDH1; indirect co-activation of ADS and DBR2 via protein-protein interaction with AabHLH1 and AabHLH113
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: JA-induced integrative regulator that links GST initiation and artemisinin biosynthesis; essential positive node coordinating both trichome density and pathway gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA treatment; developmental gradient from young to mature leaves
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: glandular-secreting trichomes (GSTs) in buds and young leaves
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates AaHD1, CYP71AV1 and ALDH1 directly; enhances AabHLH1-dependent activation of ADS; enhances AabHLH113-dependent activation of DBR2; RNA-seq also showed correlated changes in AaGSW2 and AaTAR2 downstream of AaHD1
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: AaHD1; CYP71AV1; ALDH1; ADS (indirect via AabHLH1); DBR2 (indirect via AabHLH113)
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced by MeJA; highly expressed in young buds and young leaves; expression declines with leaf age; localized to GSTs
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced by MeJA; highly expressed in young buds and young leaves; expression declines with leaf age; localized to GSTs
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Double CaMV35S promoter for overexpression; native promoter pAaMYC3 used in GUS assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases GST density by 1.39-1.53-fold and artemisinin by 1.43-1.81-fold, while RNAi reduces GST density by 17-38% and artemisinin by 27-39%.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression and RNAi in A. annua; GST density counting; HPLC metabolite analysis; RT-qPCR; RNA-seq (PRJNA1134801); subcellular localization; GUS reporter; dual-luciferase; Y1H; Y2H; BiLC; Co-IP; EMSA; CUT&Tag-qPCR; IP-MS
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Yield gain in artemisinin appears moderate compared with overexpressing some artemisinin-specific positive regulators; simultaneous boosting of GST density and pathway activity may not produce fully additive yield gains.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for molecular design breeding and metabolic engineering to coordinately raise GST density and artemisinin biosynthesis; likely best deployed in gene stacking or precision editing strategies with other pathway regulators.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaMYC3 is a JA-induced nuclear bHLH TF expressed mainly in GST-rich young tissues. 2. AaMYC3 positively regulates both GST density and artemisinin accumulation in transgenic A. annua. 3. AaMYC3 directly activates AaHD1 to initiate GST development. 4. AaMYC3 directly activates CYP71AV1 and ALDH1 and indirectly promotes ADS and DBR2 through interaction with AabHLH1 and AabHLH113. 5. AaMYC3 is the central bridge connecting GST development and artemisinin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI PRJNA1134801; co-expression analysis using public transcriptomes PRJNA416223 (SRR6472941, SRR6472946, SRR6472948, SRR6472943, SRR6472942, SRR019548, SRR6472945, SRR019547); IP-MS

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with AaMYC3 in a co-activator complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation of ADS; enhanced by AaMYC3 interaction
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator of artemisinin biosynthesis used here as a direct partner enabling AaMYC3-mediated activation of ADS
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonic acid-responsive according to paper discussion/background
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates ADS promoter; activation is strengthened by AaMYC3
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ADS transcription and, together with AaMYC3, further boosts artemisinin pathway activation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Gene stacking with AaMYC3 may more effectively enhance ADS expression and artemisinin production than single-gene manipulation.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AabHLH1 physically interacts with AaMYC3. 2. AabHLH1 activates the ADS promoter. 3. AaMYC3 enhances AabHLH1 binding/activity on ADS, indicating co-regulation of artemisinin biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with AaMYC3 in a co-activator complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation of DBR2; enhanced by AaMYC3 interaction
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator of artemisinin biosynthesis used here as a direct partner enabling AaMYC3-mediated activation of DBR2
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonic acid-responsive according to paper discussion/background
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates DBR2 promoter; activation is strengthened by AaMYC3
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes DBR2 transcription and, together with AaMYC3, further boosts artemisinin pathway activation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: IP-MS; Y2H; BiLC; Co-IP; dual-luciferase; EMSA
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Gene stacking with AaMYC3 may improve DBR2 activation and artemisinin flux control.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AabHLH113 physically interacts with AaMYC3. 2. AabHLH113 activates the DBR2 promoter. 3. AaMYC3 enhances AabHLH113 binding/activity on DBR2, indicating co-regulation of artemisinin biosynthesis.
